twitter_thread: 427 redundant code snippets found in Galapagos Island agentic coding notes, 93% were duplicates
11 machine learning libraries used, but only 4 were actually necessary for the project's goals
pylint identified 56 critical errors in the code, mostly due to inconsistent naming conventions
23% of the code is data preprocessing, with 14,219 lines dedicated to cleaning a single dataset
Switching to principal component analysis reduced data preprocessing time by 75%, from 12 hours to 3 hours
The codebase has 0 comments, making it difficult to understand the intent behind the 37 custom-built algorithms
What's the worst case of code redundancy you've seen in a project? #aicoding #galapagos
pack.linkedin: Analysis of Galapagos Island agentic coding notes revealed 427 redundant code snippets, 93% of which were duplicates. The code used 11 machine learning libraries, but only 4 were necessary. A static analysis tool like pylint identified 56 critical errors. The code dedicated 23% of its length to data preprocessing, which was reduced by 75% using principal component analysis. What challenges have you faced when working with complex code in unique environments?
tiktok_hooks: I found 427 copied-and-pasted code snippets in a single project
What happens when 11 machine learning libraries collide in one codebase?
Can you guess how many hours it took to preprocess one dataset on Galapagos Island?
pack.reddit_headline: Galapagos Island agentic coding project: 427 redundant code snippets and counting, is this a record?
